content.problemStatement:
The country of Berland initially has NNN isolated cities, the iii-th city has a significance of AiA_iAi​. The President of Berland wants to connect all the cities. He can construct a bidirectional road of length LLL (L>0)(L > 0)(L>0) from city XXX to city YYY if (AX&AY&L)=L(A_X \& A_Y \& L) = L(AX​&AY​&L)=L, where &\&& represents the bitwise AND operator.

What is the minimum total length of roads he has to construct, in order to connect all the cities in Berland? Print −1-1−1 if it is impossible.

Note: City XXX and City YYY are said to be connected if there exists a sequence of cities C1,C2,…,CKC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_KC1​,C2​,…,CK​ (K≥1)(K \ge 1)(K≥1) such that C1=XC_1 = XC1​=X, CK=YC_K = YCK​=Y, and there exists a road from CiC_iCi​ to Ci+1C_{i+1}Ci+1​, (1≤i<K)(1 \le i < K)(1≤i<K). All the cities in Berland are said to be connected when every pair of cities in Berland are connected.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN — the number of cities.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the significance of each of the NNN cities.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum total length of roads to connect all the cities in Berland, or −1-1−1 if it is impossible.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The following roads with minimum total length can be constructed to connect Berland:

A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 111 and city 222, since (A1&A2&4)=4A_1 \& A_2 \& 4) = 4A1​&A2​&4)=4.
A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 222 and city 333, since (A2&A3&4)=4A_2 \& A_3 \& 4) = 4A2​&A3​&4)=4.
A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 333 and city 444, since (A3&A4&4)=4A_3 \& A_4 \& 4) = 4A3​&A4​&4)=4.

Test case 2: The following roads with minimum total length can be constructed to connect Berland:

A road with L=2L=2L=2 between city 111 and city 333, since (A1&A3&2)=2A_1 \& A_3 \& 2) = 2A1​&A3​&2)=2.
A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 222 and city 333, since (A2&A3&4)=4A_2 \& A_3 \& 4) = 4A2​&A3​&4)=4.
